data.title: Omni Bridgeway Responds to Proposed Statutory Tax on Legal Finance Transactions in US Budget Bill
data.description:
Omni Bridgeway said it is actively working with the International Legal Finance Association (ILFA) to inform and steer the process surrounding a draft reconciliation version of the US Budget Bill, and based on current information, the firm considers it unlikely that the legal finance specific tax provision as included in the US Senate Finance Committee draft will ultimately be enacted into law, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The draft bill was released on June 16 by the US Senate Finance Committee, and it includes a section proposing to introduce a statutory tax on legal finance transactions.

This draft bill has not been reviewed or voted on by the full US Senate. Even if it passed the Senate, the bill would need to go through multiple further uncertain political reconciliation and negotiation steps with the US House of Representatives, before being enacted into law.

If the proposed tax provision is enacted into law, under 25% of the fair value of the firm's global investment portfolio might be potentially exposed to the tax, per the filing.

Its shares fell 2% in early trading on Friday.

data.title: Semler Scientific plans Bitcoin holdings of 105,000 BTC by 2027
data.description:
Healthcare tech firm Semler Scientific Inc. says it plans to boost its Bitcoin stack by nearly 28 times in the next two-and-a-half years, growing its holdings from 3,808 Bitcoin to 105,000 Bitcoin.

Semler is aiming to hold a total of 10,000 Bitcoin (BTC) by the end of this year, then 42,000 BTC by the end of 2026, before going for its final target of 105,000 BTC by 2027, the company said on Thursday.

It plans to use equity, debt financing and operational cash flow to reach its target, and has appointed a new director to lead the plan.

Semler first purchased Bitcoin in May 2024 and said it would continue to buy and hold Bitcoin for the long term. It has since bought over 3,800 BTC and has the 13th largest Bitcoin holdings among public companies, according to BitBo data.

Semler’s plan comes amid a trend of publicly traded firms buying Bitcoin, with some prioritizing the purchases above their core business operations.

Reaching 105,000 BTC would mean Semler holds 0.5% of Bitcoin’s fixed 21 million supply. Japanese investment firm Metaplanet also announced on June 9 that it aims to purchase 210,000 Bitcoin by the end of 2027.

Joe Burnett to lead Bitcoin strategy

Semler said that it hired Bitcoin researcher Joe Burnett as its “director of Bitcoin strategy” to lead its Bitcoin-buying plan.

Burnett previously served as director of market research at Bitcoin financial services firm Unchained and earlier as head analyst at Bitcoin mining firm Blockware Solutions. 

Burnett worked at the Big Four accounting firm EY (formerly Ernst & Young) before moving into the Bitcoin industry full-time.

“The trend to adopt Bitcoin as part of corporate treasury is clearly accelerating,” he said in the announcement.

Semler may need to reconsider Bitcoin buys: VanEck

VanEck crypto researcher Matthew Sigel said on Monday that Bitcoin-buying public companies should consider sunsetting their strategies altogether if their stock prices drastically drop.

He warned that companies using large at-the-market programs to raise funds for Bitcoin purchases may face problems if their stock starts trading near its net asset value, as issuing more shares could dilute shareholder value instead of boosting it.

Sigel noted that while no public company has reached that point, Semler “is now approaching parity.” 

Shares in Semler Scientific Inc. (SMLR) are down by nearly 41% this year and are approaching a similar price as before the company started buying Bitcoin, according to Google Finance.

Semler at $177 million paper gain on Bitcoin

Semler has achieved a 287% Bitcoin yield and an unrealized gain of $177 million on its Bitcoin investments as of June 3.

It also has the fourth-highest Bitcoin per share of 0.00034 among the 130 Bitcoin-holding public companies tracked by BitcoinTreasuries.NET.

Bitcoin per share shows the direct Bitcoin exposure each share in a company offers and its potential impact on stock value.

data.title: Debt Costs Less of a Headwind to HomeCo Daily Needs REIT — Market Talk
data.description: Debt costs are unlikely to remain a headwind for HomeCo Daily Needs REIT, says UBS. That's different from how it looked at the time of the Australian mall owner's 1H result in February. Then, its hedging would have rolled off from 85% in FY 2025 to 33% and 23% in FY 2026 and FY 2027, respectively. This would have resulted in a big increase in its debt costs. "However, this has since been addressed with HomeCo Daily Needs REIT's hedging now sitting at 50%+ from FY 2026-28," UBS says. It now estimates HomeCo Daily Needs REIT's debt costs at 4.9% in FY 2026/2027 compared with a prior forecast of 5.3%. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: AIC Mines Secures $40 Million Trafigura Prepayment Facility to Expand Eloise Plant
data.description:
AIC Mines executed a $40 million prepayment facility linked to an offtake agreement for the Jericho Mine with Trafigura Asia Trading to fund the expansion of its Eloise processing facility, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The prepayment is provided by Trafigura to secure the delivery of copper concentrate with no commodity hedging requirements and has early repayment flexibility.

The offtake agreement requires delivery of at least 400,000 dry metric tonnes of concentrate from the Jericho Mine.

The facility carries an interest rate of the three-month US dollar secured overnight financing rate plus 3% per annum. The availability period extends up to 18 months from the date all conditions are satisfied.

Under the grace period of the facility, no repayment of any capital or interest is required for up to 18 months from the date on which all conditions are satisfied, the filing said.

data.title: JGB Futures Lower After Firmer Inflation Data — Market Talk
data.description: Japanese government bonds are under selling pressure following signs of persistent domestic inflation. The government said earlier that Japan's consumer price index, excluding fresh food, rose 3.7% in May from a year earlier, compared with April's3.5% increase and expectations for a 3.6% rise. Persistently high inflation is keeping market expectations intact for the Bank of Japan rate increases down the road, despite the economic uncertainty stemming from U.S. tariffs and the Middle East conflict. Ten-year JGB futures are 0.11 yen lower at Y139.22. The 20-year JGB yield is up half a basis point at 2.365%. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Recover Investment Losses: Levi & Korsinsky Files Class Action Against Organon & Co. (OGN)
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / June 19, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Organon & Co. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/organon-co-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=153644&wire=1&utm_campaign=24

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Organon & Co. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between October 31, 2024 and April 30, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: According to the complaint, defendants provided overwhelmingly positive statements to investors while, at the same time, disseminating materially false and misleading statements and/or concealing material adverse facts concerning the true state of Organon's priorities, particularly, related to capital allocation through quarterly dividends. Notably, defendants concealed the high priority of Organon's debt reduction strategy following the Company's acquisition of Dermavant, resulting in a 70% decrease for the regular quarterly dividend.

Following this news, the price of Organon's common stock declined dramatically. From a closing market price of $12.93 per share on April 30, 2025, Organon's stock price fell to $9.45 per share on May 1, 2025, a decline of more than 27% in the span of just a single day.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Organon stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/organon-co-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=153644&wire=1&utm_campaign=24 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Centuria Capital May Need to Raise More Equity to Grow — Market Talk
data.description: Can Centuria Capital deliver significant growth in assets under management without raising more equity? UBS thinks it's unlikely. Still, the bank notes the environment for raising equity is improving. Centuria Capital has managed to grow pockets of assets under management, such as in agriculture, shopping centers and private credit. But the pace of growth has slowed relative to history. "As asset values stabilise and interest rates reduce over time, Centuria Capital should be in a better position to raise equity for new funds," UBS says. The bank expects progress to be modest, but it should reflect an improvement after recent challenging years. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: US Futures Decline as Trump Considers Strike on Iran
data.description:
US stock futures fell on Friday amid rising geopolitical tensions, as reports emerged that President Donald Trump is weighing a potential military strike on Iran.

The White House stated on Thursday that a final decision could come within the next two weeks, adding to investor unease amid the escalating conflict between Israel and Iran.

Markets were also digesting the Federal Reserve’s latest policy stance after it held interest rates steady earlier this week.

Fed Chair Jerome Powell adopted a cautious tone, citing heightened uncertainty due to geopolitical risks, Trump’s new tariffs, and their unclear impact on inflation.

The Fed’s updated projections now signal two rate cuts in 2025, alongside weaker growth expectations and a slightly higher inflation outlook.

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite remain modestly higher for the week, while the Dow Jones is slightly in the red.

data.title: Gold steady as Israel-Iran conflict escalates, US involvement uncertain
data.description:
Gold held steady on Friday, with geopolitical tensions escalating in the Middle East as Israel and Iran continued their air war, while investors remained wary of possible U.S. involvement.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Spot gold was steady at $3,367.60 an ounce, as of 0020 GMT. Bullion was down 1.9% so far this week.

* U.S. gold futures were also stable at $3,384.20.

* The conflict in the Middle East intensified on Thursday when Israel bombed Iran's nuclear sites, while Iran fired missile and drone strikes on Israel, including an overnight attack on an Israeli hospital. Neither side has signalled an exit strategy.

* U.S. President Donald Trump will decide in the next two weeks whether the U.S. will get involved in the Israel-Iran air war, the White House said on Thursday, raising pressure on Tehran to come to the negotiating table.

* Meanwhile, U.S. special envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi have spoken by phone several times since Israel began its strikes on Iran last week, in a bid to find a diplomatic end to the crisis, three diplomats told Reuters.

* Trump reiterated his calls for the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates, saying the rates should be 2.5 percentage points lower.

* The Fed held rates steady on Wednesday, and policymakers retained projections for two quarter-point rate cuts this year.

* European officials are increasingly resigned to a 10% rate on "reciprocal" tariffs being the baseline in any trade deal between the U.S. and the European Union, five sources familiar with the negotiations said.

* The U.S. dollar index fell 0.2%, making greenback-priced bullion more affordable for overseas buyers.

* Elsewhere, spot silver was steady at $36.36 per ounce, platinum fell 0.7% to $1,297.89, while palladium was down 0.4% to $1,046.71. All three metals were headed for weekly gain.

DATA/EVENTS (GMT)

0600

data.title: Magnetic Resources Reaches Agreement with Native Title Body for Western Australia Project
data.description:
Magnetic Resources reached an agreement with the Wangkatja Tjungula Aboriginal Corporation, the registered native title body corporate for the Nyalpa Pirniku People, over the Lady Julie project development in Western Australia, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The agreement, which includes an upfront payment and production-based royalties, removes objections to mining lease applications and enables the company to conduct activities on project tenements, the filing said.

data.title: Aether Global Innovations Appoints New Director
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

Vancouver, B.C. – TheNewswire - June 19, 2025 – Aether Global Innovations
Corp. (Frankfurt: 4XA) (WKN# A2N8RH),
a leader in drone management and automation,is pleased to announcethat it has appointed Adam Emes to the Company’s Board ofDirectors.

Mr. Emes is a seasoned entrepreneur with extensiveexperience in capital markets, corporate development and investorrelations. He is the Co-Founder of Black Swan Solutions Inc., where hehas worked with over 80 clients across various industries to drivegrowth, enhance market visibility, and lead impactful investormarketing campaigns. Mr. Emes’ expertise in digital strategy andcapital raising has supported numerous successful financings andcorporate initiatives across North America and Europe. “I’mexcited to join Aether Global Innovations at a pivotal time in itsdevelopment,” commented Mr. Emes. “The company is built on astrong foundation with a clear path for growth. I’m excited to bringmy experience to the table to help drive new opportunities, supportexecution on the ground, and deliver real value forshareholders.” 

The Company believes that the appointment of Mr. Emeswill enhance Aether’s ability to engage with the investmentcommunity and support its strategic initiatives goingforward. 

About Aether Global InnovationsCorp.

Aether Global Innovations Corp. is an innovative UAVand drone management and operations services company that focuses onthree areas for critical infrastructure and large public and privatefacilities: (i) drone management and surveillance monitoring; (ii)automation and integration for flight planning, new innovative sensorpayloads and stand-alone power sources; and (iii) drone base stationinfrastructure and technology for autonomous self-landing, powercharging, and takeoff.

For more information, please consult the Company’sfilings, available at www.sedarplus.ca, and the Company’s website, www.aethergic.com.

ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD OFDIRECTORS

Philip Lancaster, CEO & President

Aether Global Innovations Corp.

info@aethergic.com

Forward Looking Statements

data.title: RBA Could Delay Further Rate Cut Until August — Market Talk
data.description: While Australia's May employment data saw a fall in jobs growth, the data is unlikely to shift the dial for the Reserve Bank of Australia, which considers the job market to be relatively tight, says Westpac economist Ryan Wells. Westpac remains comfortable with its view that the RBA's next rate cut is most likely to occur in August, he adds. The RBA is cognizant of the downside risks to global and domestic growth, but has also made it clear it wants to adjust policy in a cautious and predictable manner. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

2036 ET - While Australia's May employment data saw a fall in jobs growth, the data is unlikely to shift the dial for the Reserve Bank of Australia, which considers the job market to be relatively tight, says Westpac economist Ryan Wells. Westpac remains comfortable with its view that the RBA's next rate cut is most likely to occur in August, he adds. The RBA is cognizant of the downside risks to global and domestic growth, but has also made it clear it wants to adjust policy in a cautious and predictable manner. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

2026 ET - The Philippine central bank is expected to continue cutting rates this year, with inflation set to stay low, Capital Economics senior Asia economist Gareth Leather writes in a note. BSP's decision to cut its policy rate on Thursday came as no surprise. The dovish tone from the central bank's statement and press conference suggests further easing is likely, he adds. "The economy could certainly do with a boost," he says. Sequential 1Q GDP growth slowed sharply. Low inflation and falling interest rates will also offer some support to demand this year, he says. Capital Economics expects another 50 bp cut from the BSP by year-end.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

2019 ET - Japan's Nikkei Stock Average is up 0.2% at 38577.17 as gains in chip stocks help offset losses in real-estate and heavy-industry shares. Kioxia Holdings gains 5.6% and Advantest is 2.2% higher, while Mitsui Fudosan falls 1.1% and IHI is 2.2% lower. Broader market index Topix is down 0.1% at 2790.44. USD/JPY is at 145.26, little changed from 145.25 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments related to the Israel-Iran conflict and its implications for energy prices. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

2004 ET - South Korea's fiscal stimulus could lift 2025 growth by 0.3 percentage point, Goldman Sachs economists Irene Choi and Goohoon Kwon write in a note. The government's latest second supplementary budget proposal that entails a KRW14.9 trillion net increase in expenditures could add 0.1 percentage point to headline GDP growth this year, on top of the 0.2 percentage point added by the first extra-budget package, they project. South Korea's economy has been forecast to grow 0.8% in 2025, compared with 2024's 2.0% growth, according to the latest forecasts from the Bank of Korea and state think tank Korea Development Institute, which have recently downgraded their 2025 growth outlooks. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

1942 ET - Japanese stocks may trade rangebound as uncertainty continues over the Israel-Iran conflict and its implications for energy prices. Nikkei futures are flat at 38510 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 145.30, compared with 145.25 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are closely watching any developments in the Middle East. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 1.0% to 38488.34 on Thursday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1110 ET - Dollar rises as air strikes between Israel and Iran raise market concerns and drive up demand for safe-haven assets."Ongoing Israel-Iran missile attacks and the possibility of U.S. involvement have increased [market] worries," IG's Axel Rudolph says in a note. The dollar DXY index rises to 99.061 after it touched a high of 99.157 earlier in the day. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0938 ET - Optimism among Canadian small-business owners continues to rebound after hitting a tariff-fueled historic low in March. The closely watched Business Barometer, published by the Canadian Federation of Independent Business, indicates the confidence index rose to 47.3 in June from 40.1 in the prior month. The reading below 50 suggests a greater share of business owners expect weaker sales over the next 12 months. Still, the June reading is well above the 25.5 record low registered in March, or when trade uncertainty was at a peak. "Early signs point to small businesses getting used to tariffs and finding alternatives," says the CFIB's chief economist, Simon Gaudreault. (paul.vieira@wsj.com, @paulvieira)

0933 ET - The Bank of England is likely to cut interest rates in August if data shows weakness in firms' pricing power and declining consumer demand, Berenberg's Andrew Wishart says in a note. Increased labor costs are, nonetheless, likely to keep U.K. inflation up, limiting BOE interest rate cuts in 2025, Wishart says. "We stick with our forecast that the BOE will hold bank rate at 4.25% until end-2025 before reducing it by 50 basis points in 2026," he says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0906 ET - The Bank of England's policy decision Thursday is not a game changer for sterling, TD Securities strategists say in a note. "There isn't a lot for sterling to chew on besides the marginally dovish vote." The BOE voted 6-3 to leave rates unchanged with three officials preferring a 25 basis points rate cut. The market was expecting a 7-2 vote for unchanged rates. Nevertheless, central banks are playing "second fiddle" to geopolitical and macroeconomic developments, particularly given concerns the U.S. is preparing to strike Iran, the strategists say. "That means the dollar is a tide that lifts or sinks all boats so not much to get excited here for sterling on the BOE." (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0859 ET - The Bank of England could cut interest rates once more in 2025, Pantheon Macroeconomics economists say in a note. The U.K. labor market data shows weakness, and wage growth is likely to fall below the monetary policy committee's expectations in the second quarter, the economists say. Potential rise in energy prices, nonetheless, risks pushing up inflation and limiting further interest rate cuts this year, they say. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0858 ET - The Bank of England is showing little sign it is about to pick up the pace of easing, despite the speed of the U.K.'s labor-market loosening, ING economists say in a note. Wage growth will likely be materially lower by the end of this year, they say. But that doesn't seem to mean faster interest-rate cuts. Hawks at the BOE will have "a beady eye" on oil prices, the ING economists say. Though the rise hasn't made much difference to the inflation outlook, the bank is wary of a repeat of 2022, when rising energy prices turned into a much wider and more persistent services-driven inflation episode, they say. ING's base case is the next rate cuts in August and November. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

0852 ET - Sterling's muted reaction to the Bank of England's policy decision Thursday makes sense, Monex Europe's Nick Rees says in a note. The BOE voted 6-3 to leave its key interest rate at 4.25%, as expected, with three members preferring a 25 basis points rate cut. Sterling is trading largely unchanged relative to levels seen before the decision, having quickly unwound an initial decline. This is because the BOE maintained its guidance for a gradual approach to future rate cuts, Rees says. "With few other notable changes in the policy statement, the lack of sterling reaction is understandable." Sterling rises 0.1% to $1.3435, compared to $1.3426 before the decision. The euro falls 0.1% to 0.8538 pounds, compared to 0.8541 beforehand.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Contact Levi & Korsinsky by August 8, 2025 to Join Class Action Against PepGen Inc. (PEPG)
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / June 19, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your PepGen Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/pepgen-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=153643&wire=1&utm_campaign=10

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against PepGen Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between March 7, 2024 and March 3, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (i) the Company's lead product candidate, PGN-EDO51, was less effective and safe than defendants had led investors to believe; (ii) phase two, CONNECT2 study was dangerous or otherwise deficient for purposes of U.S. Food and Drug Administration ("FDA") approval; (iii) as a result of all the foregoing, PepGen was likely to halt the CONNECT2 study, and PGN-EDO51's clinical, regulatory, and commercial prospects were overstated; and (iv) as a result, defendants' public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in PepGen Inc. stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/pepgen-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=153643&wire=1&utm_campaign=10 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

data.title: FOS Capital Meets Acquisition Conditions for Aldridge Traffic Systems
data.description:
FOS Capital confirms that all conditions precedent for the acquisition of Aldridge Traffic Systems, including vendor releases from secured creditors, have been satisfied, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Settlement of new shares under the placement is expected on June 23, with allotment and trading of the new shares anticipated on June 24, the filing added.

data.title: BOJ board agreed to keep rate hike path, some saw need for pause, May minutes show
data.description:
Bank of Japan policymakers agreed on the need to keep raising still-low interest rates, but some saw the need to pause for the time being due to uncertainty over U.S. trade policy, minutes of their April 30-May 1 policy meeting showed on Friday.

At the meeting, the BOJ kept interest rates steady at 0.5% but sharply cut its growth and inflation forecasts due to the expected hit on Japan's economy from higher U.S. tariffs.

While the board agreed to keep raising interest rates if the economy resumes a recovery, some said the timing for Japan to see underlying inflation converge toward the BOJ's 2% target has been pushed back by around a year, the minutes showed.

The board was divided on the future inflation path.

While some warned that cheap Chinese imports could push down Japan's prices, others said inflation could overshoot the BOJ's projections as companies have become more willing to raise prices and wages, the minutes showed.

U.S. President Donald Trump's sweeping tariffs appeared to dominate discussions with some board members predicting the BOJ would enter a holding pattern until there was more clarity on Washington's trade talks including with Japan.

"The BOJ must maintain a wait-and-see approach until developments in U.S. tariff policy become somewhat settled," one member was quoted as saying.

Another member said the BOJ would pause for now but shouldn't be too pessimistic and stand ready to resume rate hikes "in response to policy changes in the United States," the minutes showed.

data.title: Shandong Senter Electronic Opens Books For 640 Million Yuan Shenzhen IPO
data.description:
Shandong Senter Electronic (SHE:001388) has started taking subscriptions on Friday for its 640.4 million yuan initial public offering on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange's ChiNext board.

The Chinese electrical equipment manufacturer is marketing up to 39 million shares priced at 16.42 yuan apiece, according to a filing late Thursday.

The company will trade under stock code 001388 and stock name Senter Electronic.

data.title: Australian Clinical Labs Names Stephen Roche as Chair
data.description:
Australian Clinical Labs appointed Stephen Roche as board chair, effective Aug. 26, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Mark Haberlin, chair of the audit and risk committee, will continue as interim chair until Aug. 26, the filing added.

Roche has over 15 years of experience as a director of public companies, not-for-profits, and private family offices, having held executive roles as the managing director and chief executive of Australian Pharmaceutical Industries and Bridgestone Australia & New Zealand, per the filing.

The company's shares were up almost 1% in recent Friday trade.
